sanskrit: तस्मादसक्तः सततं कार्यं कर्म समाचर । असक्तो ह्याचरन्कर्म परमाप्नोति पूरुषः ॥
hindiTranslation: इसलिये तू निरन्तर आसक्तिरहित होकर कर्तव्यकर्मका भलीभाँति आचरण कर क्योंकि आसक्तिरहित होकर कर्म करता हुआ मनुष्य परमात्माको प्राप्त हो जाता है ॥
englishTranslation: Therefore, always perform your duty without attachment; by acting without attachment, a person reaches the supreme.
essence: Freedom comes when action is done without grasping at its reward.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Keep doing what must be done, but do it without clinging to results. That kind of action leads you to the highest good.
simpleInsight: There is relief in giving your full effort without carrying the weight of outcomes.
detailedMeaning: On Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen between duty and fear. Krishna has just described the person who needs nothing from action. Now he turns that insight into practice: act steadily, but do not cling. That freedom itself becomes the way forward.
modernRelevance: You send the message, make the hard apology, or submit the work knowing it may not land how you hope. The act is complete even before the response arrives.
translationLiteral: तस्मात् (therefore) / असक्तः (without attachment) / सततम् (always) / कार्यम् (duty) / कर्म (action) / समाचर (perform fully) / असक्तः (without attachment) / हि (indeed) / आचरन् (acting) / कर्म (action) / परमम् (the supreme) / आप्नोति (reaches) / पूरुषः (a person)
insight: It shifts attention from owning the result to offering the action cleanly.

quoteVariants.text: Do your duty without clinging. That is how you reach the highest.
The work is yours; the grip on results is not.
Act without clinging. Reach the highest.
